content: Asymptomatic hyperuricemia: generally NOT recommended to treat pharmacologically per ACR and EULAR guidelines, as most never develop gout and risks of lifelong ULT may outweigh benefits. Exceptions where treatment considered: serum urate >13 mg/dL (men) or >10 mg/dL (women) with high risk of tumor lysis syndrome; before starting chemotherapy for hematologic malignancies; recurrent uric acid kidney stones; patients receiving rasburicase for tumor lysis prevention. Lifestyle modifications for all: weight loss if overweight/obese, limit alcohol (especially beer and spirits), reduce high-purine foods (organ meats, certain seafood), avoid fructose-sweetened beverages, increase low-fat dairy intake (uricosuric effect), stay well-hydrated, consider coffee consumption. Review and adjust medications contributing to hyperuricemia if alternatives available (e.g., switch thiazide to losartan which has mild uricosuric effect). Treat comorbidities aggressively.
